title: lightly-larded-peruvian-aubergine-tortilla
description: a peruvian twist on the classic tortilla, featuring roasted aubergine and air-fried to perfection.
instructions: preheat the air fryer to 375 degrees fahrenheit. prepare the aubergine by cutting it into 1-inch thick slices and placing them on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper.
roast the aubergine in the oven at 425 degrees fahrenheit for 15 minutes, or until the skin is blistered and charred.
while the aubergine is roasting, cook the pancetta in a skillet over medium heat until crispy. remove from heat and set aside.
in the same skillet, cook the diced onion and minced garlic until softened. add the diced tomatoes and cook for an additional 2-3 minutes.
in a large bowl, whisk together the eggs, quinoa flour, and a pinch of salt and pepper.
add the cooked aubergine, cooked pancetta, and tomato mixture to the bowl with the eggs. stir until well combined.
heat the olive oil in a non-stick skillet over medium heat. pour in the egg mixture and cook until the edges start to set.
use a spatula to gently lift and fold the edges of the tortilla towards the center, allowing the uncooked egg to flow to the edges.
once the tortilla is almost set, use the spatula to carefully fold the tortilla in half and cook for an additional 1-2 minutes.
remove the tortilla from the skillet and let it cool for a few minutes before slicing and serving.
